terms.definition: Maps a vector of logits to class probabilities π_k = exp(z_k) / Σ_j exp(z_j), parameterizing a Categorical distribution at the output layer.
A vector π = (π_1,…,π_K) with π_k > 0 and Σ_k π_k = 1, representing mutually exclusive class likelihoods; softmax is the standard way to produce them from logits.
Numerically stable computation of log Σ_j exp(z_j) as m + log Σ_j exp(z_j − m) where m = max(z); prevents overflow when evaluating softmax and log-softmax.
log π_k = z_k − log Σ_j exp(z_j), computed via log-sum-exp; preferred input to categorical cross-entropy loss for stability.
Unbounded scores z_k output by the final linear layer before softmax; larger z_k yields larger π_k but logits are not probabilities until normalized.
Cross-entropy where Q is parameterized by softmax(logits); standard output layer for multi-class neural networks.
